findings.message: plugin.json has no `commands` array; the commands/ directory is present in the repo but may not be auto-discovered by Claude Code's plugin installation path, leaving commands unregistered for plugin installs
commands/ and skill/commands/ contain identical copies of all seven command files; copy-skill.js only reads from skill/commands/, making commands/ an orphan that can silently drift on future edits
Command file declares no `allowed-tools` frontmatter; uses Read (trace file) and Bash (git add + git commit in step 7)
"well-structured" is a vague qualitative term with no measurable definition in this context
"concise summary derived from trace summary" — "concise" is unmeasured; the trace summary itself is already constrained to 2-3 sentences
Command file declares no `allowed-tools` frontmatter; uses Read (manifest files), Write (.codetape/ structure), and Bash (directory creation via MkDir)
"likely component roots" — "likely" is vague; no criterion given for what makes a root more or less likely

All command files reference @references/* and @templates/* paths that only resolve after the skill is installed; commands used standalone (before install) will silently skip these context docs with no error message
Command file declares no `allowed-tools` frontmatter; uses Read (trace files in .codetape/traces/)

Command takes an optional query argument ($ARGUMENTS) but provides no fallback behaviour for invocation with no arguments; the skill/SKILL.md routing section says to show all traces, but the command file itself is silent
Command file declares no `allowed-tools` frontmatter; uses Read (component-map) and Write (docs/ARCHITECTURE.md)
Command file declares no `allowed-tools` frontmatter; uses Read (component-map, config) and Write (drift.json)
Command file declares no `allowed-tools` frontmatter; uses Read (config, traces) and Write (sync targets)
"appropriate template" in step 4c — "appropriate" is undefined; the mapping is actually deterministic (README→readme_section.md, etc.)
Command file declares no `allowed-tools` frontmatter; uses Read (config, component-map), Bash (git diff), and Write (trace file, component-map update)
skill/SKILL.md has no YAML frontmatter; `name` field is absent, breaking skill registration and display in Claude Code
skill/SKILL.md has no YAML frontmatter; `description` field is absent, blocking skill summary in plugin registries and @ autocomplete
Identical to commands/trace-commit.md — no `allowed-tools` frontmatter declared
"well-structured" is a vague qualitative term (identical file to commands/trace-commit.md)
"concise summary" — unmeasured qualifier (identical file to commands/trace-commit.md)
Identical to commands/trace-init.md — no `allowed-tools` frontmatter declared
"likely component roots" — vague (identical file to commands/trace-init.md)
Identical to commands/trace-log.md — no `allowed-tools` frontmatter declared
Identical to commands/trace-log.md — no empty-input fallback described
Identical to commands/trace-map.md — no `allowed-tools` frontmatter declared
Identical to commands/trace-review.md — no `allowed-tools` frontmatter declared
Identical to commands/trace-sync.md — no `allowed-tools` frontmatter declared
"appropriate template" — vague (identical file to commands/trace-sync.md)
Identical to commands/trace.md — no `allowed-tools` frontmatter declared
skills/codetape/SKILL.md (npm-distributed copy) has no YAML frontmatter; `name` field absent, affecting all users who install via npx codetape install
skills/codetape/SKILL.md (npm-distributed copy) has no YAML frontmatter; `description` field absent
CORS wildcard header on /api/data and /events endpoints allows any browser tab to cross-origin-fetch local trace data from the running dashboard server
exec() used to open browser with template string; both variables are controlled (hardcoded platform command + parseInt'd port) so exploitation is not feasible, but exec() spawns a shell unnecessarily
External mermaid.js loaded from cdn.jsdelivr.net at major version @10 without Subresource Integrity hash; CDN compromise would inject malicious JavaScript into the local dashboard

skills/codetape/SKILL.md (npm-distributed copy) has no YAML frontmatter; `name` field absent, affecting all users who install via npx codetape install
skills/codetape/SKILL.md (npm-distributed copy) has no YAML frontmatter; `description` field absent
